zh.Arborea, Sardinia, Italy: 義大利薩德尼亞阿波亞
zh.Arborea, Oristan, Sardegna, Italy: 義大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾阿波亞

zh.lifeSummary: 當Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea於1080年出生在義大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾阿波亞時，他的父親Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea16歲，母親Ana De Zorri26歲。他和Hélène de Orru至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。他去世於1144年之後在義大利。

zh-hans.Oristano, Sardinia, Italy: 意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea于1080年出生在意大利薩德尼亞奧利斯塔諾阿波亞时，他的父亲Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea16岁，母亲Ana De Zorri26岁。他和Hélène de Orru至少有2个儿子和2个女儿。他去世于1144年之后在意大利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: locative name from Lacon in Wem (Shrops). This name has probably been confused with or absorbed by   Lakin   (1), especially in counties adjoining Shrops, such as Staffs.

2 English: while the name in Yorks could be that in (1) through migration, its appearance in late 14th-century Clint in the north-west of WR Yorks, and the persistent final -can in most early forms of the surname, suggest that it may have had a different but unknown origin.

variants.name: Laken
meanings: 1  English:  see  Lakin  .
2  English:  see  Lacon  .

variants.name: Laking
meanings: English:  see  Lacon   and   Lakin  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

nameMeanings.name: Serra,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Eduardo, Francisco, Jorge, Mario, Enrique, Jaime, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon.  Italian Antonio, Angelo, Enrico, Guido, Salvatore, Amedeo, Elio, Marco, Nicolo, Sandro, Caesar, Carlo.  Portuguese Henrique, Joaquim.

Italian, Portuguese, and Catalan: topographic name from Italian, Portuguese, Catalan serra ‘ridge or chain of hills; mountain range’ (from Latin serra ‘saw’), or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, in Italy e.g. Serra d'Aniello and Serra Pedace (Cosenza), Serra San Bruno (Vibo Valentia), and Serracapriola (Foggia), in Valencia and Catalonia (Spain) e.g. Serra d'Almos or Serra d'en Galceran.
Catalan (Serrà): topographic name for someone who lived by a sierra, from Catalan serrà, an adjective derived from serra (see 1 above), the same as Castilian Serrano .

variants.name: Serritella
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Amelio, Angelo, Carmela, Rocco, Romeo, Santa.
Italian: possibly a topographic name related to   Serra  .

variants.name: Sera
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Armando, Fernando, Jose, Alberto, Alvaro, Ana, Assunta, Elia, Enrique, Ernesto, Evelio.  Japanese Yoshio, Hideo, Hitomi, Katsumi, Kenji, Kimihiro, Masao, Mikio, Nobuhiro, Sadao, Takeo, Tsuneo.
Portuguese, Galician, and Catalan: most probably a variant of   Serra  .
Italian (Séra): from a short form of a personal name with this ending, such as Baldissera, Bonasera or Serafino .

variants.name: Serret
meanings: Catalan and southern French: topographic name from a diminutive of Catalan   Serra   and Occitan   Serre  .

variants.name: Serrano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Pedro, Carlos, Miguel, Francisco, Jesus, Jorge, Ricardo, Rafael.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim, Wenseslao.

Spanish: topographic name for someone who lived by a mountain ridge or chain of hills, from an adjectival derivative of serra (see Serra ). This surname is also found in the Philippines, Portugal, and Brazil.
Italian (in Calabria Serranò): habitational name for someone from any of various places called Serra (see Serra ). The surname Serrano may also be of Spanish origin.

nameMeanings.name: Comita
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carlo, Attilio, Carmelo, Carmine, Cosmo, Domenic, Gaspare, Larraine, Vito.

Italian: from medieval Latin comitus ‘count’ or the medieval Greek form of this word, komitos, applied as a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces or for someone in the service of a count.

variants.name: Comis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Greek Spiro.

Greek: from medieval Greek komis ‘count’, from Latin comes (see Comito ), attested as a surname in the 13th century. However, the modern surname is more likely a Hellenisation of the Italian surname Comi , which is derived from the same medieval Greek word.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea was born in 1080, in Arborea, Oristano, Sardinia, Italy, his father, Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea, was 16 and his mother, Ana De Zorri, was 26. He had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Hélène de Orru. He died after 1144, in Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea est né en 1080, à Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Italie, son père, Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Ana De Zorri, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Hélène de Orru. Il est décédé après 1144, à Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea 1080 in Arborea, Oristano, Sardinien, Italien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea 16 und seine Mutter Ana De Zorri 26. Er hatte mit Hélène de Orru mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb nach 1144 in Italien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea è nato il nel 1080 nel Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, il suo padre, Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Ana De Zorri, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Hélène de Orru. Morì dopo il 1144, nel Italia.

ja.Arborea, Sardinia, Italy: イタリアサルデグナアルボアア
ja.Arborea, Oristan, Sardegna, Italy: イタリアサルデグナオリスタノアルボアア
ja.Oristano, Sardinia, Italy: イタリアサルデグナオリスタノ
ja.lifeSummary: Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arboreaが1080年にイタリアサルデグナオリスタノアルボアアで生まれた時，父Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arboreaは16歳，母Ana De Zorriは26歳であった。彼はHélène de Orruとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼は1144年以後にイタリアで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea(이)가 1080년 이탈리아 사르데그나 오리스탠오 아볼이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Ana De Zorri(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Hélène de Orru(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1144년 이후 이탈리아에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea nasceu em 1080, em Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Itália, seu pai, Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Ana De Zorri, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Hélène de Orru. Ele faleceu após 1144, em Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea родился в 1080 г. в местности арбореа, ористано, сардегна, Италия, его отцу по имени Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Ana De Zorri, 26. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Hélène de Orru. Он умер после 1144 г. в местности Италия.

sm.from 1131 to 1147: mai le 1131 e o'o I le 1147

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Comita II de Lacon Serra, Judge of Arborea nació en 1080, en Arborea, Oristano, Sardegna, Italia, su papá, Constantino I de Lacon Gunale, Judge of Arborea, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Ana De Zorri, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Hélène de Orru. Murió después de 1144, en Italia.
